chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 696 of 2018 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case For hearing of main case 15-03-2022 Mr. Mehboob Ali Wassan, Assistant Advocate General Sindh..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- None present on behalf of the Petitioner nor any intimation is received. It appears that the Petitioner is seeking appointment with the Respondents in lieu of a plot, which was donated by his father for the purpose of a Primary School though his father was also given a job as Chowkidar against the said donation, who has now been retired. The Petitioner has relied upon Form VII of the said plot, service book of his father and an application for the post of Chowkidar, which has not been entertained by the Respondents. In our considered view, no right could be accrued on the basis of an application to file instant Petition. The only claim of the Petitioner is that Petitioner’s father had donated a plot to the Respondents, and on that basis, he is entitled for appointment. As to this, the Hon’ble Supreme Court in Civil Appeals No. 19-K to 50-K of 2015 (Re: Government of Sindh and others v. Loung Khan Rajper and others) vide order dated 09-11-2015 has already held that no appointment can be given on plot donation. In view of hereinabove facts and circumstances, no case for indulgence is made out, and therefore, the Petition is dismissed. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
